channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: 'Indefensible' and 'unacceptable' - furious Rosenior questions Chelsea's desire
item.description: Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior strongly criticises his players after they suffer a fifth loss in a row without scoring for first time since 1912.

item.title: Real Madrid's Tchouameni on Man Utd radar
item.description: Real Madrid's Aurelien Tchouameni is one of the players Man Utd are looking at to fill their priority central midfield position this summer.

item.title: Rangers captain Tavernier to leave in summer after 11 years
item.description: Captain James Tavernier announces he will leave Rangers at the end of the season, after 11 years at Ibrox.

item.title: Leicester fans demand change as Rowett urges action after relegation
item.description: Leicester City fans demand change at the club after relegation to League One, while manager Gary Rowett says "decisive action" is needed.

item.title: 50 days until World Cup - pick your favourite kits
item.description: BBC Sport has highlighted some of the most interesting kits which will be seen at this summer's World Cup. Pick your favourites.

item.title: 'Turning football into rubble' - fury over VAR ruling
item.description: A referee has to be escorted off the pitch by police after a controversial VAR decision in the Asian Champions League semi-finals sparks fury.

item.title: Cucurella's barber and the 'leaked' Chelsea team news
item.description: The apparent leak of Chelsea team news before Tuesday's defeat at Brighton is being linked to posts reportedly made on social media platform X by Marc Cucurella's barber.

item.title: Football quiz: Name every winner of Championship
item.description: Premier League-bound Coventry City have secured the Championship title. Can you name every winner from the past 22 seasons?

item.title: McTominay & Gilmour on Scotland's World Cup hopes
item.description: Napoli duo Scott McTominay and Billy Gilmour target a first for Scotland at a World Cup this summer.

item.title: Spain defender Batlle close to agreeing Arsenal move
item.description: Spain defender Ona Batlle is close to agreeing a move to Arsenal as a free agent in the summer.

item.title: Chelsea to play all WSL games at Stamford Bridge
item.description: Chelsea say switching all their women's games to Stamford Bridge reaffirms an "ambition and intent" to become "the biggest women's sports club in the world".

item.title: Fernandes would win Ballon d'Or at Man City - Pogba
item.description: Paul Pogba believes Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes would be in contention to win the Ballon d'Or if he played for a team like Manchester City.

item.title: Brighton beat Chelsea to heap more pressure on Rosenior
item.description: Brighton heap more pressure on Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior as they ease to a comfortable 3-0 win thanks to goals from Ferdi Kadioglu, Jack Hinshelwood and Danny Welbeck.

item.title: Things need to change after 'unacceptable' defeat - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior says his side's 3-0 defeat away at Brighton was "unacceptable" from everyone involved and that things need to change.

item.title: 'People can say whatever stupid things they want' - Guardiola on celebrations
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his players can "celebrate however you want" in response to criticism from some pundits for his side's reaction to beating Arsenal on Sunday.

item.title: Watch the goals and celebrations as St Johnstone clinch title
item.description: St Johnstone win 2-0 at Dunfermline Athletic to wrap up the Scottish Championship title with two games to spare.

item.title: Brighton 'dominated' game in win over Chelsea - Hurzeler
item.description: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler says he was impressed by the "intensity" of his side as they cruised to a dominant 3-0 win over Chelsea to boost their European hopes.

item.title: How Leicester went from Premier League champions to League One in a decade
item.description: Ten years ago, Leicester were days away from winning the Premier League. Next season, they will play in League One. How?

item.title: Wrexham relax into tension of Championship play-off race
item.description: After beating Oxford United to edge back into the Championship play-offs on goal difference, have Wrexham found form and composure at just the right moment?

item.title: 'Most enjoyable season since Wright years' - how Saints stormed to title
item.description: How St Johnstone stormed Scottish Championship title to make immediate Premiership return, with an emphatic start paving their path to glory.

item.title: Hearts' McEntee & Magnusson to miss rest of season
item.description: Manager Derek McInnes says Hearts must "deal with" losing Oisin McEntee and Tomas Magnusson for the rest of the season.

item.title: Kolo Muani on Liverpool's radar - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Liverpool could move for Randal Kolo Muani, Kobbie Mainoo agrees to new Manchester United deal and Bernardo Silva is on Arsenal and Barcelona's radar.

item.title: Players can celebrate how they want - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his players can "celebrate however you want" in response to criticism for their reaction to beating Arsenal.

item.title: Lingard becomes first British scorer in Brazilian cup
item.description: Former England forward Jesse Lingard becomes the first British player to score in Brazil's domestic cup, according to his club Corinthians.

item.title: Can Arsenal's season be compared to McIlroy's 2025 Masters?
item.description: The Monday Night Club debate whether Arsenal's season is comparable to McIlroy's 2025 Masters.

item.title: West Ham earn valuable point at London rivals Crystal Palace
item.description: West Ham edge a point clearer of the relegation zone as they share a goalless draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Who is Iraola successor Rose?
item.description: German football journalist Constantin Eckner provides the background on Bournemouth's incoming head coach Marco Rose.

item.title: 'It will go all the way' - Nuno on relegation battle after draw at Palace
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo belives the Premier League relegation battle "will go all the way" after his side's 0-0 draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Palace not clinical enough in West Ham draw - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says his side were "not clinical enough" in their 0-0 Premier League draw with West Ham at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: 'I owe the city' - Bellingham buys stake in Birmingham Phoenix
item.description: England midfielder Jude Bellingham buys a 1.2% stake in cricket team Birmingham Phoenix, saying "I feel like I owe the city something".

item.title: How Haaland and Gabriel went head-to-head in 'old school battle'
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Wayne Rooney and Danny Murphy discuss how "two top players" in Erling Haaland and Gabriel went head-to-head in Manchester City's 2-1 victory against Arsenal.

item.title: Bayern beat Stuttgart to seal 35th Bundesliga title
item.description: Bayern Munich come from behind to secure a 4-2 victory against Stuttgart and win their 35th German Bundesliga title with four games to spare.

item.title: How Liverpool's improved creative play helped them beat Everton
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy analyses how Liverpool's performance "got better" from a "creative" and "footballing aspect" as The Reds secured a 2-1 victory at Everton in the Merseyside Derby.

item.title: Man City beat Arsenal to close gap in title race
item.description: Erling Haaland and Rayan Cherki strike as Manchester City beat league leaders Arsenal to close the gap to three points with a game in hand and pile more pressure on Mikel Arteta's side.

item.title: Arteta rues missed Arsenal chances in loss at Man City
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says his side had "the best chances in the game" against Manchester City.

item.title: Van Dijk's 100th-minute winner settles Merseyside derby
item.description: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk scores a 100th-minute winner to secure an important victory against Everton in the first Merseyside Derby at The Hill Dickinson Stadium.

item.title: Villa score late to beat Sunderland in seven-goal thriller
item.description: Tammy Abraham's late winner helps Aston Villa survive a late Sunderland fightback and give Unai Emery's side a crucial 4-3 victory in their quest for Champions League football next season.

item.title: Championship clubs reject bringing in VAR next season
item.description: Championship clubs have turned down the chance to introduce a challenge system of VAR for the 2026-27 season.

item.title: 'Every kid's dream' - third-choice keeper Woodman on life at Liverpool
item.description: Liverpool's Freddie Woodman on the "tough" life of a third-choice goalkeeper and what it was like being thrown in "at the deep end" in the Merseyside derby.

item.title: Ranking the most successful world record transfers of the past 50 years
item.description: Stumping up world record sums doesn't always guarantee success, but plenty of football's most expensive players have delivered on their hefty fees - here are the top 10.

item.title: Sutton's predictions - midweek games including Burnley v Man City
item.description: Chris Sutton, the BBC readers and AI make predictions for this week's Premier League fixtures on Tuesday and Wednesday.

item.title: Why stakes are so high in England's hottest title race
item.description: BBC Sport explores the hottest title race in English football, and examines why the consequences are so big for the teams involved.

item.title: Brady steps down as West Ham vice-chair
item.description: Baroness Karren Brady steps down as vice-chair of Premier League side West Ham after 16 years.

item.title: Midtjylland's Djabi seriously injured in stabbing
item.description: Midtjylland midfielder Alamara Djabi is in a stable condition having been seriously injured in a stabbing in Denmark, his club says.

item.title: Spurs lifeline or good step for West Ham - who's favourite for drop?
item.description: With five games remaining, BBC Sport looks at which side is most likely to take the third relegation spot.

item.title: Give our Premier League top assisters quiz a go
item.description: The list for most assists in a single Premier League season is so full of legends it inspired us to build this quiz.

item.title: Why Wolves' relegation had been coming after slow decline
item.description: Wolves have been relegated from the Premier League after just three wins in their first 33 games of the season.

item.title: 'By chance or choice, they have clicked' - why Man City will be champions
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy explains why he thinks Manchester City will beat Arsenal to the Premier League title.

item.title: Record-breaking Bayern win Bundesliga - now for the Treble
item.description: Free-scoring Bayern Munich swept to the German title for the 35th time. Now their sights turn towards completing the Treble.

item.title: Ten days to decide second place in the Championship?
item.description: Four clubs are locked in a battle to secure the second promotion spot in the Championship.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who can still receive a yellow card suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Celtic's Engels attracts Lazio interest - gossip
item.description: Celtic midfielder attracts Italian interest as Rangers urged to sign goalkeeper.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Brentford to host World Sevens as six WSL teams confirm participation
item.description: Aston Villa, Manchester United, Tottenham Hotspur and West Ham will compete in this year's Women's World Sevens tournament, which will be hosted by Brentford's Gtech Community Stadium.

item.title: Wales legend Fishlock to retire at end of NWSL season
item.description: Seattle Reign announce Fishlock to retire at end of current NWSL season

item.title: Arsenal forward Blackstenius signs new contract
item.description: Arsenal forward Stina Blackstenius signs a new two-year contract with the Women's Super League club.

item.title: Bailie ready for 30th and final season at Glentoran
item.description: Glentoran defender Kelly Bailie reflects on her 30-year association with the club as she prepares for her final season as a player before retirement.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
